codeReview.summary: Reviewed the worker's verification-only handoff against the cited commit and the round3-relevant code paths. The budget regression is addressed by reusing the shared company admission-block helper in `server/src/services/budgets.ts`, which lets stubbed tests surface the budget-owned company pause without a second failing company lookup, and scheduler admission now short-circuits on company lifecycle state before the generic budget rejection path so the updated scheduler wording matches runtime behavior. I found no high-confidence implementation bugs in the round3-specific behavior covered by this feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill does not describe a normal success path for a verification-only session where the assigned feature is already satisfied on the checked-out commit, so the worker had to report `followedProcedure: false` even though revalidation was the correct action.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff `/Users/aischool/.factory/missions/082135e3-5840-4875-806d-1c1e9e82e66a/handoffs/2026-04-08T03-49-17-981Z__stabilize-runtime-lifecycle-baseline-round3__e9190503-0983-4a25-88d0-1563ef97716e.json` says "No additional code edits were required" and records a deviation for the implementation/edit phase; `.factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md` steps 3-4 assume tests-first followed by implementation edits.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature-to-commit traceability is weak for no-edit completions: the handoff points scrutiny at a later broad admission-gating commit rather than a round3-specific change set, which makes feature-scoped review harder than it needs to be.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The reviewed feature description in `/Users/aischool/.factory/missions/082135e3-5840-4875-806d-1c1e9e82e66a/features.json` names only `budgets-service.test.ts`, `scheduler.test.ts`, and `agent-instructions-routes.test.ts`, but the handoff commit `fed40a243f7e3bb6f5861a1be2924a1f1be8ad2f` (`git show --stat`) touches 17 files dominated by broader lifecycle admission work.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the feature metadata, both round3 handoffs, the worker transcript skeleton for session `e9190503-0983-4a25-88d0-1563ef97716e`, the backend-worker skill, and the cited commit diff. The round3-specific budget/scheduler stabilization looks correct, and I found no actionable code defects in that scope; I only noted shared-state gaps around verification-only handoffs and feature-to-commit traceability.
